content: Session management pitfalls in SaaS with Better Auth: (1) Ghost sessions — server-side session record not invalidated on logout; clearing client cookie alone is insufficient; attacker who captured token before logout retains access; (2) Password change without invalidating all sessions leaves compromised sessions active; (3) Privilege change (plan upgrade, 2FA enabled) without session reissuance means old sessions operate at old privilege level; (4) Unlimited concurrent sessions increases credential stuffing and account sharing risk; (5) Token rotation race: concurrent requests can both present the same refresh token, causing one to fail; must atomically check-and-rotate; (6) Better Auth revokeSession must be called server-side, not just cookie-cleared; (7) Session timeout must be enforced server-side — client-side idle detection only is bypassable; (8) Admin revocation of session must propagate immediately — cached session data in Next.js server components causes stale auth.
